title: podman 5.6.0-rc1 has problems with hardlinks
body:
### Issue Description

With podman 5.5.2 i was building successfully my own bootc-based images for fedora (42 and rawhide)
Especially the qcow-creation (using quay.io/centos-bootc/bootc-image-builder:latest) and the `bootc upgrade` on my target atomic-os machines was running flawlessly.

This weekend, podman 5.6.0-rc1 came up in the testing-repos of fedora.

I am still experimenting with bootc, so i removed all images from the local (rootful) image cache and started building my images freshly.
This all looked good until i wanted to deploy with bootc or with the bootc-image builder.

The error messages are all the same kind, with varying 'problematic files': (here the logs of bootc-image-builder)

```
Finished module org.osbuild.mkfs.btrfs

Starting module org.osbuild.bootc.install-to-filesystem

Finished pipeline image
manifest - failed
Output:
Failed
```

To pin down the problem, i downgraded selectively until this result:
`dnf downgrade podman-5:5.5.2  buildah-2:1.40.1`

I removed all my rootful images again, rebooted and started fresh.

Voila, everything working again, no complaint of image-builder or bootc

Btw, i know that it's not necessary to run as root when building the images, but for the bootc-image-builder step. This was only convenience for me to stay rootful..

On a similar gitlab pipeline, i built the images rootless (but privileged) with the same success on podman 5.5.2

So, both approaches worked with 5.5.2
Testing on 5.6.0-rc1 has only been done rootful

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. install podman 5.6.0-rc1
2. build new bootc-based images
3. try to install... failure with hardlinks

Fix:
downgrade to podman 5.5.2


### Describe the results you received

see above

### Describe the results you expected

podman 5.6.x being compatible with 5.5.2 and bootc-images

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: @nalind could it be a duplicate of https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/6297 ?
Yes, I think it is, granted only after a quick skim of the code.
To be clear this is an ostree bug, it should handle this case https://github.com/ostreedev/ostree/issues/3489 - but yes we should just update buildah.

I doubt that ostree's the only software that looks at archives without writing them to a filesystem and which doesn't expect to be following a chain when resolving hard links.  tar(5) could be read to say that this is invalid.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
This was fixed for the 5.6 branch for rc2 by #26752.
